summary: A rectangular multi-tower structure with four tall corner towers and a lower central block between them. The central block is layered with alternating horizontal bands and has exterior walkways/balconies; towers are brick-faced with stone/stonebrick vertical corner trims and overhanging slab roofs. A raised central pathway runs along the roofline connecting towers. The build sits on a simple flat ground pad with white slab/blocks forming a perimeter and entrance steps.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main mass with four tall rectangular towers at corners and a central raised walkway
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long axis (approximate)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof edging and broad horizontal coping

materials.name: slab_wood
materials.role: dark top roof surface (possible nether_brick slabs)

materials.name: fence
materials.role: small decorative window grills or railings

rebuild_hints: Place four identical square towers near each corner of a rectangular base; towers are ~3 blocks wide internally with stonebrick vertical corner columns and brick insets with small windows every few blocks.

Build a lower central rectangular block between towers with three horizontal floor bands of alternating brick and lighter block (possibly sandstone or quartz) and continuous narrow glass bands near middle floors.
Add white slabs/blocks around the ground edge as a raised perimeter/footing and a central entrance projection with short white steps.

Construct flat overhanging slab roofs on towers: a dark 3x3 to 5x5 inner slab area with lighter slab edging (stone slabs) extending one block beyond the tower walls; place a light source (glowstone) centered on each tower roof.
Run a raised central stonebrick walkway across the top of the central block, aligned with the long axis, connected to tower roofs by short bridges.
uncertainties: Exact block types for lighter horizontal bands: they appear beige/white (could be sandstone, smooth sandstone, or quartz) so material listed as uncertain.
Dark top roof material is likely nether_brick or dark wood slabs — identification uncertain (slab_wood assigned with moderate confidence).
Interior floor layouts and exact floor counts inside towers are not visible; floors_estimate is based on external banding only.
Exact footprint dimensions are approximate from perspective images; width/depth/height are rounded to nearest integer and may be off by several blocks.
